messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2026 Streaming
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,
Can't join EuroBioC2026 in person? We've got you covered.
All conference sessions will be streamed live and are free to attend online. Each session room has its own streaming link, so be sure to use the appropriate link for the session you would like to follow.
You can find all streaming links here:
https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/pages/streaming.html#streaming
Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Dear Bioconductor community,
Can't join EuroBioC2026 in person? We've got you covered.
All conference sessions will be streamed live and are free to attend online. Each session room has its own stream

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Landing page does not match the current version of my package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Landing page does not match the current version of my package
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I don't think the message is being allowed through because of the images referenced/included. The bioc-devel mailing list does not allow image attachments.

For others can reference the original question:
Why do the landing page versions of my package at bioconductor.org  do not match the checktable at Runiverse page?

We do not use R-universe yet as the default build for packages; that is in process. We still have our linux builders
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/.  We pull windows and some mac products from R-universe but compared to the passing version of our linux builder as there are certain Bioconductor criteria that is being checked and processed on the linux builders that we have not yet implemented on R-universe only builds.

The build reports and landing pages are lagging as there was an issue with the post processing infrastructure on our end.  We apologize that we did not announce this more broadly on the mailing list for reference and to avoid confusion; we will make sure to do this in the future.  We have implemented a fix and expect the builds to resume and update within the next 24 hours.  Thank you for your patience and understanding.

We appreciate your diligence to the Bioconductor builds and clarification question.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.body_plain:
Dear BioC,

We would like to change the maintainer for the MoonlightR package from me to Antonio Colaprico (antonio.colaprico at curie.fr<mailto:antonio.colaprico at curie.fr> ; cc?d).

What do we need to do to make this happen? I assume we need confirmation form Antonio at the very least (he should already be part of BioConductor)

I know I?m supposed to request the change here, but I am a bit fuzzy on the details:

  1.
I assume we need to change the maintainer role in the DESCRIPTION file - does this need to be done immediately both in the release and devel branch? And before or after the official change?
  2.
Once you do the switch, will the change be effective for both branches immediately?

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo

Thanks a lot for your time

Best

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for reaching out.

We can proceed with this request as the current maintainer.  Please have Antonio confirm and we can make this change on the configuration on our end.

We recommend Antonio make the change once we finish up change the configuration as then they can test that change of access was successful.

Once the configuration on our end is changed, it is immediate on both branches.


Antonio,
I do not see a BiocCredentials account yet for you.  We will need to create this in order to update the configuration.  Please let us know what email you would like to use for login and your github username. We will send activation email directly to you. Once activated you will be able to manage ssh keys for access.


Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Matteo Tiberti via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Tuesday, June 9, 2026 3:25 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Cc: Colaprico Antonio <antonio.colaprico at curie.fr>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Change of maintainer for MoonlightR package


Dear BioC,

We would like to change the maintainer for the MoonlightR package from me to Antonio Colaprico (antonio.colaprico at curie.fr<mailto:antonio.colaprico at curie.fr> ; cc?d).

What do we need to do to make this happen? I assume we need confirmation form Antonio at the very least (he should already be part of BioConductor)

I know I?m supposed to request the change here, but I am a bit fuzzy on the details:

  1.
I assume we need to change the maintainer role in the DESCRIPTION file - does this need to be done immediately both in the release and devel branch? And before or after the official change?
  2.
Once you do the switch, will the change be effective for both branches immediately?

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo
